heroHeadline: Iran Re-Closes Hormuz; Trump Threatens to Destroy Iran's Power Plants and Bridges
heroSubtitle: Iran reversed course on April 19, re-closing the Strait of Hormuz just two days after declaring it open, turning back LPG tankers and citing US 'breaches of trust.' Trump escalated with a stark threat to 'knock out every single Power Plant, and every single Bridge, in Iran' if no deal is reached by the April 22 ceasefire deadline. Vice President Vance, Steve Witkoff, and Jared Kushner are heading to Islamabad for emergency negotiations — Iran's delegation arrives Tuesday, with both sides hoping to announce a ceasefire extension by Wednesday. Gasoline is averaging $4.05 nationally and Energy Secretary Chris Wright warned prices may not fall below $3/gallon until 2027. Trump also signed a short-term FISA Section 702 surveillance extension through April 30.
footerNote: Data sourced from White House, Federal Register, executive orders, court records, Al Jazeera, CNN, Reuters, AP, NPR, Bloomberg, CNBC, Axios, NBC News, CBS News, Fox News, Gulf News, The National, Newsweek, Nate Silver/Silver Bulletin, YouGov/Economist, Fox News, BLS, Peterson Institute for International Economics, Tax Foundation, CFR, EIA, Treasury, Goldman Sachs, Federal Reserve, OECD, Cato Institute, Penn Wharton Budget Model.
